claim: Gene expression can be highly variable across genetically identical cells.

result.claim: Gene expression can be highly variable across genetically identical cells.
result.reasoning: The claim that gene expression can be highly variable across genetically identical cells is strongly supported by multiple propositions from different papers. Paper 524574 states that "Gene expression divergence has been detected within populations of genetically identical cells" (prop_629). Similarly, paper 4320300 notes that "Genetically identical cells produce variable levels of a given protein at a given time" (prop_1299). Paper 1028111 further elaborates that "Genetically identical cells exposed to the same environmental conditions can show marked differences in phenotypic characteristics" (prop_678) and "variation in molecular content" (prop_677), linking this to stochasticity in gene expression. Paper 12804937 reinforces this by stating that "Cell-to-cell variations in mRNA and protein levels are caused by randomness in transcription and translation" (prop_2686) and that "Stochastic gene expression appears in organisms ranging from microbes to metazoans" (prop_2687). The consistency across these sources, all directly addressing the variability of gene expression in genetically identical cells, provides strong evidence. While the citation counts and publication years are unknown for these specific entries, the directness and agreement of the propositions are compelling.

evidence.text: Intra-genotypic variability describes phenotypic variation among genetically identical individuals.
evidence.source: The Evolution of Variance Control

evidence.text: Some cell lines up-regulated their CD44v6 expression when the same cell lines formed tumors after s.c. injection into severe combined immunodeficient mice.
evidence.source: Regulation of CD44v6-containing Isoforms during Proliferation of Normal and Malignant Epithelial Cells

evidence.text: Genetically identical cells exposed to the same environmental conditions can show variation in molecular content.
evidence.source: Stochasticity in gene expression: from theories to phenotypes

evidence.text: Gene expression divergence has been detected among different strains.
evidence.source: Conservation of Expression and Sequence of Metabolic Genes Is Reflected by Activity Across Metabolic States

evidence.text: Genetically identical cells sharing an environment can display markedly different phenotypes.
evidence.source: Memory and Modularity in Cell-Fate Decision Making

evidence.text: Stochasticity in gene expression can be advantageous.
evidence.source: Stochasticity in gene expression: from theories to phenotypes

evidence.text: Studies of inbred mice and monozygotic twins show that variation in the epigenotype can be seen even between genetically identical individuals.
evidence.source: How lifetimes shape epigenotype within and across generations

evidence.text: Cell-to-cell variations in mRNA and protein levels are caused by randomness in transcription and translation.
evidence.source: Nature, Nurture, or Chance: Stochastic Gene Expression and Its Consequences

evidence.text: The CRY1 variant causes reduced expression of key transcriptional targets.
evidence.source: Mutation of the Human Circadian Clock Gene CRY1 in Familial Delayed Sleep Phase Disorder

evidence.text: Genetically identical cells exposed to the same environmental conditions can show marked differences in phenotypic characteristics.
evidence.source: Stochasticity in gene expression: from theories to phenotypes

evidence.text: Genes with a high range of optimal flux levels tend to display divergent expression levels among different yeast strains and species.
evidence.source: Conservation of Expression and Sequence of Metabolic Genes Is Reflected by Activity Across Metabolic States

evidence.text: The typical cell authentication method using short tandem repeat (STR) markers may not detect cellular heterogeneity.
evidence.source: Genetic variability in a frozen batch of MCF-7 cells invisible in routine authentication affecting cell function

evidence.text: Differences in MCF-7 cells were traced back to genetic heterogeneity using Comparative Genomic Hybridization (CGH).
evidence.source: Genetic variability in a frozen batch of MCF-7 cells invisible in routine authentication affecting cell function

evidence.text: CD44v6 expression was up-regulated in cell lines.
evidence.source: Regulation of CD44v6-containing Isoforms during Proliferation of Normal and Malignant Epithelial Cells

evidence.text: Stochastic gene expression appears in organisms ranging from microbes to metazoans.
evidence.source: Nature, Nurture, or Chance: Stochastic Gene Expression and Its Consequences

evidence.text: Gene expression divergence has been detected within populations of genetically identical cells.
evidence.source: Conservation of Expression and Sequence of Metabolic Genes Is Reflected by Activity Across Metabolic States

evidence.text: Gene expression is regulated across the cell cycle in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.
evidence.source: Model-Based Deconvolution of Cell Cycle Time-Series Data Reveals Gene Expression Details at High Resolution

evidence.text: Stochastic gene expression has consequences for cellular function.
evidence.source: Nature, Nurture, or Chance: Stochastic Gene Expression and Its Consequences

evidence.text: Gene expression divergence has been detected among closely related species.
evidence.source: Conservation of Expression and Sequence of Metabolic Genes Is Reflected by Activity Across Metabolic States

evidence.text: Genetically identical cells produce variable levels of a given protein at a given time.
evidence.source: Variability and memory of protein levels in human cells
